PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSE TO FORM INTERROGATORIES AND TO COMPEL FURTHER ANSWERS TO SPECIALLY PREPARED INTERROGATORIES, REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, AND REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED. DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO RESPOND AND FAILURE TO RESPOND FULLY WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. Notice Of Motion To Reconsider Order Of May 20, 2005
Off Calendar Aug-05-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Paul Talcott Currier appearing in pro per, 571-1583.

Ruling:
TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's Motion To Reconsider Order of May 20, 2005 is denied. There are no new facts or different law offered in support of the motion. As Defendant contends that a statement of damages is required for the entry of an order striking the answer, Defendant is incorrect. Any contention regarding the lack of a statement of damages goes to the entry of a money judgment. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Paul Talcott Currier, appeared in Propria Persona, Plaintiff/moving party
Ricardo Saria, appeared in Propria Persona, Defendant and opposing party

Ruling:
Hearing held and Tentative Ruling adopted: Plaintiff's Motion For Sanctions For Disobedience Of Court Order is granted as it seeks terminating sanctions. No opposition has been filed. The court finds that additional orders to respond to discovery would not result in compliance. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
